title: papaver libanoticum subsp. libanoticum
intro: Papaver libanoticum subsp. libanoticum, also known as the Syrian poppy, is a plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) and is native to the Middle East. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its alkaloid content, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Papaver libanoticum subsp. libanoticum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
